data.title: Crypto Exchange CEO's Daughter, Grandson Targeted in Paris Kidnap Attempt
data.description:
The daughter and grandson of a French crypto exchange CEO were targeted in a failed kidnap attempt, adding to the growing number of crypto-related kidnapping cases in the country, France 24 reported on Tuesday.A video widely shared on social media showed the attempt, in which three masked men wrestled the woman and another person to the ground in Paris. A shop owner intervened, waving a fire extinguisher, and the attackers gave up, driving off in two waiting vans. France 24 identified the crypto exchange as Paymium
source: https://www.coindesk.com/business/2025/05/15/crypto-exchange-ceos-daughter-grandson-targeted-in-paris-kidnap-attempt

data.title: Coinsilium Group raises £1.25 million via ordinary share placement to buy Bitcoin
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Web3 investment institution Coinsilium Group announced that it has raised £1.25 million through the placement of ordinary shares to purchase Bitcoin, further investment and general working capital. In addition, the company will also choose a Bitcoin financial strategy. (Sharesmagazine)

data.title: Core Inflation Indicator Expected to Reach Lowest Level Since 2021
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the core inflation indicator favored by the Federal Reserve is anticipated to drop to its lowest level since 2021 in April's data. Economists from Pantheon Macroeconomics and Capital Economics estimate that the core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) annual rate, excluding volatile food and energy prices, could decrease from 2.6% in March to 2.5% in April. This would mark the lowest level since March 2021, just before the pandemic-induced inflation surge began.

Previously, BlockBeats reported that Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell indicated the April PCE might be around 2.2%.

data.title: Report Reveals Tether’s Blacklisting Isn’t Instant — $78M Moved Illegally
data.description:
TL;DR A report from AMLBot reveals that Tether delays enforcing blacklists, allowing $78 million in USDT to be moved before the funds were frozen. The delay stems from Tether’s multi-signature approval process, giving malicious actors a time window to evade the freeze. Tether states it collaborates with over 255 agencies across 55 countries and has. . . 
source: https://crypto-economy.com/report-reveals-tethers-blacklisting-isnt-instant-78m-moved-illegally/

data.title: The temperature check proposal to include PEPE as a supported asset on the Aave platform was passed with 76.76% support
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the AAVE community governance proposal, the temperature check proposal to use PEPE as an asset supported by the Aave platform has been passed with a support rate of 76.76%. The proposal recommends introducing PEPE as collateral in the isolated market into Aave V3, aiming to expand the user base, promote the growth of GHO stablecoins, and increase the protocol liquidation income. The risk service provider will set the risk parameters in the future and enter the ARFC and AIP process.

data.title: Hong Kong Police Force: CryptoTrace, a virtual asset analysis tool, has been deployed
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The Cyber ​​Security and Technology Crime Bureau (CTCB) of the Hong Kong Police Force announced the security maintenance of Hong Kong's virtual asset ecosystem. It pointed out that Chief Superintendent Lam Cheuk-ho emphasized the importance of exchanges, regulators and law enforcement agencies to share responsibilities. In addition, the CTCB cooperated with the University of Hong Kong to develop the virtual asset analysis tool CryptoTrace, providing advanced intelligence and investigation support for criminal investigation units. In addition, the New Territories South Regional Anti-Fraud Team was established.

data.description:

source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/dot-slides-after-delay-in-polkadot-etf/

data.title: Proposed GENIUS Act Targets Stablecoin Issuance and Tech Companies
data.description:
According to PANews, a draft of the GENIUS Act has been revealed, outlining several key regulatory measures for stablecoin issuers. The draft aims to prohibit misleading claims of FDIC insurance by stablecoin issuers and the use of terms associated with the U.S. government in their naming conventions. Additionally, it seeks to prevent non-financial tech companies, such as Meta and Google, from issuing stablecoins, reinforcing the separation between banking and commerce.

The proposed legislation also aims to enhance enforcement capabilities against violations and expand ethical oversight for special government employees, including figures like Elon Musk. These measures are part of a broader effort to regulate the stablecoin market and ensure transparency and accountability among issuers and related entities.

data.title: Sonic Labs will not renew its contract with market maker Wintermute
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Sonic Labs strategy director assistant.sonic announced on the X platform that it would not renew the contract with market maker Wintermute, ending the five-year exclusive partnership between the two parties. The Sonic team will turn to market makers that can provide "MM++" services, and expects new partners to not only provide centralized trading platform support, but also actively participate in the construction of the DeFi ecosystem and support application growth.

data.title: Starknet Teams Up with Lombard to Add Bitcoin Liquid Staking to DeFi
data.description:
TL;DR Starknet added LBTC to its network, a 1:1 Bitcoin-backed token created by Lombard Finance for liquid staking. The integration lets users access DeFi applications using Bitcoin without moving funds from its original blockchain, expanding available tools. The network was also recognized as a Stage 1 ZK Rollup, reinforcing its security and decentralization within the. . . 
source: https://crypto-economy.com/starknet-teams-up-with-lombard-to-add-bitcoin-liquid-staking-to-defi/

data.title: He Yi introduces the Binance Sprout Program: Open to the world, encouraging applicants from diverse backgrounds to join
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Binance co-founder He Yi introduced the latest talent recruitment project "New Sprout Program" in the AMA, which will find and cultivate talents who are passionate about the blockchain and cryptocurrency industry. He Yi said that Binance not only pays attention to the technical background of candidates, but also attaches importance to innovative thinking, rapid learning ability, enthusiasm for industry development and desire for success. The "New Sprout Program" is open to applications from all over the world and is suitable for young people from different backgrounds and development stages. Applicants from diverse backgrounds are encouraged to join and jointly promote the development of the blockchain ecosystem.
